column8: I (to do) my homework by six o’clock tomorrow.
column9: I (to do) all my homework by the time he comes.
column10: I (to do) my homework by the time you come.
column11: How many pages ... you ... (to read) by five o’clock tomorrow?
column12: ... you ... (to do) this work by next Sunday?
column13: I (to translate) this letter by 6 o'clock this afternoon.
column14: I (to make) this doll by her birthday.
column15: By the end of the month the comission (to come) to some decision.
column16: He (not to learn) his lesson by tomorrow, if he has not yet begun to study it.
column17: This work is so hard, that I (not to complete) it in a year's time.
column18: After you finish this book, you (to learn) over a thousand words.
column19: I ... already ... (to go) to the South by that time.
